summary: Draft closed PR focused on src/melee/ty/tylist.c and related ty headers, with small formatting-only edits in gm and gr files. The diff typed several TyList structures and rewrote trophy-list code around TyListArg/TyListState fields, HSD_JObj helpers, arrays, loops, and literals. Bot output reported two new tylist function matches and several improvements, but also nine regressions including tylist functions and .data/.sdata sections; no human review comments were present.

key_files.role: Primary decomp source edit: added local TyList helper structs, rewrote trophy-list functions, replaced expanded JObj translate/dirty code with helpers, changed archive symbol string handling, added includes.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: tylist.c includes sc/forward.h and baselib/aobj.h

key_files.role: Type recovery for TyListArg and TyListState fields; converted individual x0/x4/x8, xC/x10/x14, and text slots into links/jobjs/texts arrays and refined several field types.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: types.h TyListArg links[3], jobjs[3], texts[3]
raw_diff: types.h TyListState pointer type and padding updates

key_files.role: Header prototype updates for recovered tylist function signatures.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: tylist.h un_8031305C signature changed to TyListState* plus s8

key_files.role: Small formatting-only cast/compound literal spacing change.
Small formatting-only function pointer cast spacing change.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: gmClassic_801B3500 function pointer cast spacing

key_files.role: Small formatting-only function pointer cast spacing change.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grIceMt_801FA364 function pointer cast spacing

what_changed: Changed six files: tylist.c, tylist.h, ty/types.h, gm_18A5.c, gmclassic.c, and gricemt.c.
In tylist.c, added local structs such as TyListArchiveAnimData, TyListWaitData, TyListCameraState, TyListDispEntry, and TyListDispState.
In ty/types.h, changed TyListArg from separate unknown pointer/JObj slots to grouped links[3], jobjs[3], and texts[3], while preserving the 0x34-byte size comment.
Updated tylist.h prototypes to use TyListArg* and revised TyListState-based signatures instead of void* or extra parameters.
Rewrote several tylist functions from generated-looking gotos, pointer arithmetic, and expanded JObj dirty/assert code into arrays, for loops, helper calls, and local variables.
Replaced some string literals for archive names and symbols with offsets from un_803FDD18 in un_803124BC and un_8031263C.
Bot report claimed new matches for un_8031263C and un_803124BC, improvements for un_80313774, .sdata2, un_803147C4, and un_80313508, and regressions for nine tylist items including .data and .sdata.

smart_moves.text: Grouping related TyListArg slots into arrays made repeated access patterns clearer: links[3], jobjs[3], and texts[3] replace several xN fields and support indexed loops.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: tylist.c un_80312E88_inline loops over arg->jobjs[i]

smart_moves.text: Expanded HSD_JObj translate/dirty/assert sequences were collapsed to HSD_JObjSetTranslateY where the operation was evident.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_80312BAC removes direct translate.y/__assert/HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub block and calls HSD_JObjSetTranslateY

smart_moves.text: Header prototypes were kept aligned with recovered function bodies instead of leaving void* signatures.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: tylist.h un_80312904 and un_8031305C prototype updates

decomp_lessons.lesson: When adjacent struct slots are repeatedly accessed by index or stride, grouping them into arrays can expose natural loops and reduce pointer arithmetic, but the field layout still needs corroboration.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_80312E88_inline for loop over 3 jobjs

decomp_lessons.lesson: Expanded JObj helper bodies in decompiled output should be checked against canonical HSD_JObj helper calls before preserving assert/dirty boilerplate.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_80312BAC replaces expanded translate/dirty code with HSD_JObjSetTranslateY

decomp_lessons.lesson: A PR can improve or match individual functions while still causing nearby function and data-section regressions; intake should keep the match gains and regression ledger together.
evidence_refs: bot issue comment: 9 regressions including .data and .sdata

naming_conventions.text: Recovered local role names include archive, entry, state, jobj, parent_jobj, pos_x, pos_y, pos_z, idx, show, move_x, and move_y.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: tylist.c un_80312904 local variable names

naming_conventions.text: Partially understood struct fields remained address-style or xNN-style, while clearer repeated slots were renamed to links, jobjs, and texts.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: types.h TyListArg links[3]/jobjs[3]/texts[3] plus x24/x25/x28/x2C/x30

assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: Replaced while/goto-style repeated logic with for loops over entryCount or fixed count 3 where the diff showed indexed iteration.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_80312BAC for (i = 0; i < state->entryCount; i++)

assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: Used HSD_JObjSetTranslateY instead of inlined translate.y plus dirty-matrix/assert boilerplate.
Updated tylist.h declarations after recovering TyListArg/TyListState usage.

The PR replaced several archive and symbol string literals with un_803FDD18 offset expressions; bot output later reported .data and .sdata regressions, so this tactic should be treated as risky rather than accepted.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_803124BC data + 0x608/0x614 and symbol offsets

notes: PR body was empty and no human review comments were present, so author intent and reviewer intent are not supported.
The PR was closed with merged_at null and marked draft in raw PR JSON; do not treat its changes as accepted project source.
The raw diff excerpt was truncated in loaded_files, though targeted reads recovered the tylist.h and types.h sections.
Bot output provides narrow score evidence but also reports several regressions; lessons should not be promoted as validation of the full PR.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep match gains and regression ledger together
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2454 shows that a tylist edit can create new function matches while regressing nearby tylist functions and data sections; curator should preserve both the gains and regressions when mining tactics.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl bot issue comment: 2 new matches, 4 improvements, 9 regressions

accepted_candidate_records.title: Array-shaped type recovery can enable natural loops
accepted_candidate_records.text: The TyListArg change from separate pointer/JObj/text fields into links[3], jobjs[3], and texts[3] enabled clearer loop-based tylist code in the PR slice, but needs acceptance corroboration before becoming a path fact.

source_update_candidates.title: Candidate TyListArg grouped slot interpretation
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2454 proposes that TyListArg offset 0x00 has three TyListArg links, offset 0x0C has three HSD_JObj pointers, and offset 0x18 has three HSD_Text pointers. Because the PR was closed unmerged and bot output reported regressions, this should only be adopted if later accepted source or matching evidence confirms it.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: types.h TyListArg links[3], jobjs[3], texts[3]
raw_diff: tylist.c uses arg->links, arg->jobjs, arg->texts
source_update_candidates.reason: Potentially useful local type fact, but not accepted by this PR evidence alone.

source_update_candidates.title: String literal to data-offset rewrites are risky in code matching
source_update_candidates.text: This PR replaced tylist archive/symbol string literals with un_803FDD18 offset expressions, while the bot report included .data and .sdata regressions. Existing standards already cover this risk; no new standard is needed unless more PRs show the same pattern.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_803124BC and un_8031263C string literal replacements
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful as a reinforcing example for existing standards, not as a new accepted rule.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the un_803FDD18 data+offset archive string changes as accepted style; the loaded evidence includes data-section regressions and no supporting data ownership explanation.
Do not claim the PR fully matched tylist or was merged; metadata shows CLOSED with merged_at null.
Do not infer reviewer intent; there were no loaded human review comments.
Do not treat the recovered TyListArg/TyListState field names as global/path facts without corroborating later accepted source.
